title: Database Design Patterns Quiz
description: Test your fundamental knowledge about database design patterns and data modeling in modern web applications.

questions.question: What's the primary benefit of using 'UUIDs' (Universally Unique Identifiers) as primary keys?
options: They are smaller than standard integers.
They can be generated on multiple servers without any risk of collision, which is essential for distributed systems.
A type of database indexing.
None of the above.

questions.question: What is the 'Audit Log' pattern in database design?
options: A way to encrypt data before deleting it.
Instead of overwriting data, you create a separate table to record every change to sensitive records (Who, What, When).
A type of database indexing.
A way to automatically compress files.

questions.question: Which level of 'Normalization' ensures that every cell in a table contains only a single (atomic) value and there are no duplicate columns?
options: 1NF (First Normal Form)
2NF (Second Normal Form)
3NF (Third Normal Form)
None of the above.

questions.question: What is 'Polymorphic Association' useful for?
options: When an entity (like a 'Comment') can belong to multiple different types of parents (like a 'Post' or a 'Video').
A way to encrypt files using a secret key.
To avoid using any Foreign Keys.
None of the above.

questions.question: Why is it generally better to define Constraints (like NOT NULL or UNIQUE) at the database level rather than just in your code?
options: They make the database slower.
They enforce your design rules permanently, ensuring that no application (Express app, mobile app, internal tool) can accidentally violate them.
To automatically generate a backup of the data.
To improve the user experience.
